original_hypothesis.title: Wallet Can Be Permanently Bricked by Removing All Owners
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `removeOwnerAtIndex` function deletes an owner from the wallet but does not enforce a minimum owner count. An owner can accidentally or maliciously remove all owners, including themselves. Once the last owner is removed, the wallet becomes permanently locked, as no one can authorize new transactions, add owners, or upgrade the implementation.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Smart contract wallets must maintain at least one valid authorization mechanism to prevent permanent loss of access. The lack of a minimum owner check allows the wallet to enter a dead-end state from which recovery is impossible.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Deletion of owner state without checking remaining active owners

preconditions: Wallet has one or more owners
An authorized owner executes removeOwnerAtIndex until zero owners remain

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Unrestricted deletion leading to dead-end state
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: The EntryPoint validation path relies entirely on _validateSignature, which will fail if no owners exist
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No `require(ownerCount > 1)` or similar check before deletion

analysis_questions: Is there any backdoor or recovery mechanism if all owners are removed?
Can the EntryPoint bypass the signature check in any edge case?
validation_requirements: Deploy a wallet with one owner
Call removeOwnerAtIndex to remove the sole owner
Attempt to execute any transaction or add an owner; verify it reverts

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A user's secondary device is compromised. The attacker, knowing they cannot easily extract the funds due to off-chain monitoring, decides to grief the user by submitting a UserOp that calls `removeOwnerAtIndex` for all registered owners. The transaction succeeds, and the wallet's funds are permanently locked.
prerequisites: Compromised owner key or severe user error

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Permanent lock of all assets in the wallet

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests verifying behavior when the last owner is removed

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Visible on-chain as a RemoveOwner event leaving the wallet empty

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
